title: First Rootinest Tootinest Race
wikitext:
[]: First Rootin'est Tootin'est Race             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 02, 2010 5:03 am Most of the ideas that ended up in Evangelion were named after existing mythological, psychological, and scientific concepts, but meaning very different things in NGE's universe. The Lance o' Longinus, the Angels, all that crap. But what about the never-mentioned-in-the-show-by-this-name-but-still-very-significant-to-the-plot First Ancestral Race (and the scrapped-but-considered-at-one-point Second Ancestral Race )? Yes, they too fall into that pattern. As it turns out, the Japanese "第一始祖民族", which we've been translating as "First Ancestral Race", actually refers to the First Root Race. The concept of root races was first used by Helena Petrovna Blavatsky in her 1888 magnum opus The Secret Doctrine, the Synthesis of Science, Religion and Philosophy . Sounds right up Eva 's alley already, doesn't it? Blavatsky wrote of seven root races, each of which corresponds to a continent. No, not our currently existing continents, but lost continents and continents that don't yet exist. See, two of the races have yet to appear. The Sixth Root Race will not appear until the 28th century. As Blavatsky wrote it, the First Ancestral Root Race, or Polarians, were enormous, ethereal, amorphous beings who reproduced via fission. They were "higher" spiritually than humans, and could be considered Godlike. The continent they lived on, called the "Imperishable Sacred Land", was also ethereal, and thus was not destroyed like the continents of later races (e.g. Lemuria and Atlantis). Nor did the First Root Race actually die out; they created the Second Root race, who were more solid in form, and were gradually absorbed into their progeny. Buuut that didn't work out too well for them, because the Second Root Race (the Hyperboreans) are all dead now and have no living descendants. There you go. The First Root Race in The Secret Doctrine isn't exactly like the First Ancestral Root Race in Evangelion , but most of the mythological connections in Eva are pretty tenuous. So, when do we start changing every instance of "First Ancestral Race" to "First Root Race" on the wiki?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Fri Jul 02, 2010 6:49 am Hurray, mass upheaval! Chaos! Confusion! Brothers tearing their skin from eachother's flesh to use as bibs for when they eat eachother's ribs! Tears of sorrow for me to nourish my black-heart! Change! Edit! Mutilate the word! Slice each letter down to the vertebrae and suck the marrow from their ivory heart! Slaughter them like roaches under the shampoo bottle of change! Pour peroxide on their half torn bodies and watch as the bubbles of reformation undulate from the pasty white guts of their mediocrity! In all seriousness, 'Ancestral Race' sounds a hell of a lot cooler than 'Root Race', although the accompanying concept sounds awesome and all. Why has the phrase allegedly been mistranslated this entire time?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Jul 02, 2010 9:07 am first "New Movie Edition" and now "First Root Race"? SPOILER: Show

[]: Roots are like tree feet. Rei has feet. Rei's a tree.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 02, 2010 10:17 am I... honestly can't tell whether this topic is serious or not. I mean, really. The words ancestral and root hold basically the same meaning. I agree with GasmaskAvenger. Does changing the names of completely superficial things to better reflect their raw Japanese translations make any difference? It's not like we're going to squeeze any secret meanings out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 02, 2010 11:02 am Don't tell me that Anno went for Blavatsky's peculiar brand of nuttery? Talking about root races gives me "bad RahXephon" vibes. In the absence of anything better, I'd rather stick to First Ancestral Race. Sounds cooler and more original, too. (And FRR is not nearly as fun as FAR, acronyms-wise) Oh, and if you have to talk about root races, ours is the fifth, so the older ones (three and four, at least) would have to be still around somewhere, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Jul 02, 2010 11:12 am ^Atlantis and Lemuria saw the end of the fourth and third root races, since both mythical continents are supposed to have been reclaimed by the sea ages ago. So no, they're goners.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 02, 2010 11:48 am UrsusArctos wrote: In the absence of anything better, I'd rather stick to First Ancestral Race. Sounds cooler and more original, too. (And FRR is not nearly as fun as FAR, acronyms-wise)

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Fri Jul 02, 2010 11:52 am What the heck is a root race? Dumb question, I know.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Jul 02, 2010 12:00 pm Nah, I'm cool with FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 02, 2010 12:02 pm Dark doom wrote: What the heck is a root race? Dumb question, I know.

[]: Coming soon to Fanfiction.net - Evangelion: Nightwatch             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 02, 2010 12:05 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Another name for an ancestor race at the end of the day. It's basically saying they were the source for other races.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Fri Jul 02, 2010 12:09 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Another name for an ancestor race at the end of the day. It's basically saying they were the source for other races.

[]: Re: Coming soon to Fanfiction.net - Evangelion: Nightwatch             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 02, 2010 12:10 pm Killer Bee wrote: Which is exactly what "Ancestral" implies, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 02, 2010 1:43 pm Dark doom wrote: What the heck is a root race? Dumb question, I know.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jul 02, 2010 1:53 pm We should only change their names if Zizek and Wagner and Matt Greenfield say it's ok.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 02, 2010 2:00 pm GasmaskAvenger wrote: I'm really getting sick of people claiming unintended literal translations as what should be what something is called

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 02, 2010 2:31 pm NAveryW wrote: Decide for yourself whether it's better to be correct or to sound cool.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 02, 2010 3:17 pm Sorry, but New Age bullshit is where I draw the line. I was fine with just calling the FAR "gods."

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jul 02, 2010 3:40 pm I still call them Elohim.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 02, 2010 3:47 pm I'm fine with calling them whatever people generally want to go by. It's just shorthand for 'whomever made the seeds' to me anyway, since I don't place much stock on extraneous sources.
